title: R2C2 protocol using 10x Single-Cell RNA-seq Assay cDNA
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ejm3o","text":"This protocol is based on the published Rolling Circle to Concatemeric Consensus (R2C2) method (1). The aim is to make elongated cDNA via rolling circle amplification using cDNA generated from the 10x Chromium 3' (or 5') single-cell RNA-seq assay to increase the accuracy of downstream sequencing on an Oxford Nanopore Technologies sequencing platform. The major modification from the original protocol is to add an additional cDNA re-amplification step (see section \"10x cDNA re-amplification\") to enrich for fragments with the proper sequence (poly(T) at 3' and TSO at 5') at both ends, which is essential in circularization with the 10x Splint fragment.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Prepare the following on ice %s %s
Run the following program Thermocycler Conditions: (Lid@ 105 Â°C , 50 Â”l) 95 Â°C , 00:03:00 followed by 1 cycle of: 98 Â°C , 00:01:00 62 Â°C , 00:01:00 72 Â°C , 00:06:00 then 4 Â°C , 00:00:00, hold

Cleanup with Select-a-Size columns (Zymo) and a size cut-off of ~125 bp. Follow the Select-a-Size cleanup protocol for single-size selection, but in the buffer preparation add 85 Â”L of 100% ethanol to 500 Â”L of Select-a-Size DNA binding buffer. Elute in 15 Â”L DNA elution buffer (from the kit). QC with 1/5x dilution for Quant-it (Thermo Fisher Scientific) and 1/50x dilution for BioAnalyzer DNA HS assay (Agilent). Label this product as "10x Splint", store at -20 Â°C for later use.
Enrichment PCR Prepare the follow reaction mix on ice %s %s

Run the following program Thermocycler Conditions: (Lid@ 105 Â°C , 100 Â”l) 98 Â°C , 00:00:45 followed by 10 cycle of: 98 Â°C , 00:00:10 62 Â°C , 00:00:15 72 Â°C , 00:03:00 then 72 Â°C , 00:05:00 4 Â°C , 00:00:00, hold

Circularization Prepare the following mix %s %s Incubate @ 50 Â°C for 01:00:00 Then add the following mix %s %s Incubate @ 37 Â°C for 06:00:00 , then @ 80 Â°C for 00:20:00 Cleanup the reaction with 40 Â”L SPRI beads (0.8x)
Circularization product purification
Add 40 Â”L (0.8x) of resuspended, room-temperature AMPure XP SPRI beads to the reaction mix. Pipette mix 10 times. Perform a quick spin to collect all liquid from the sides of the tube.
Incubate sample on bench top for 00:05:00 at Room temperature .
Place the tube on a magnetic stand to separate the beads from the supernatant. Use a P200 pipettor to remove the supernatant.

Wash 2 times with 200 Â”L of freshly prepared 80% ethanol. After removal of the second wash of 200 Â”L of ethanol, spin the tube strip briefly, return to magnetic stand and remove residual ethanol with a P20 pipettor. Do not let the beads dry out.

Remove the tube from the magnetic stand. Immediately add 30 Â”L of H 2 O and pipette mix to resuspend. Perform a quick spin to collect all liquid from the sides of the tube. Place at Room temperature for 00:05:00 to elute the cDNA from the beads.

Wash 2 times with 200 Â”L of freshly prepared 80% ethanol. After removal of the second wash of 200 Â”L of ethanol, spin the tube strip briefly, return to magnetic stand and remove residual ethanol with a P20 pipettor. Do not let the beads dry out.
Place the tube on a magnetic stand to separate the beads from the supernatant. Use a P200 pipettor to remove the supernatant.
Incubate sample on bench top for 00:05:00 at Room temperature .
Add 95 Â”L of resuspended, room-temperature ProNex beads to the PCR mix. Pipette mix 10 times. Perform a quick spin to collect all liquid from the sides of the tube.
cDNA purification

Remove the tube from the magnetic stand. Immediately add 15 Â”L of buffer EB (10 mM Tris-HCl, pH 8.5) and pipette mix to resuspend. Perform a quick spin to collect all liquid from the sides of the tube. Place at Room temperature for 00:05:00 to elute the DNA from the beads.
Place the tube on a magnetic stand to separate the beads from the supernatant. Transfer the eluted cDNA samples to a new tube.
QC with 1/5x dilution for Quant-it and BioAnalyzer DNA HS assay. Store the remaining product at -20 Â°C for later use.
Place the tube on a magnetic stand to separate the beads from the supernatant. Transfer the eluted cDNA samples to a new tube.
Prepare the following mix, then split into 3 reaction wells of 50 Â”L each. %s %s Incubate in a thermocycler @ 30 Â°C for Overnight

SPRI clean and T7 endonuclease treatment Pool all reactions and adjust volume to 300 Â”L with H 2 O. Add 150 Â”L 150 Â”L SPRI beads (0.5x) to your DNA and leave on a rotator for 5 minutes to mix. If not homogeneous, flick the tube to mix. Notes: After adding the AMPure XP SPRI beads and mixing by inverting the tubes, there's uneven distribution of the beads, mostly the sign of longer cDNA strand. Place on a magnet stand until a complete pellet forms, wash twice with 500 Â”L 70% ethanol. After the ethanol removal, add the following mix to re-suspend the beads. %s %s Incubate on a thermal shaker at 37 Â°C at 1000 RPM for 02:00:00 . Agitate the tube occasionally to help the solution homogenize. Place on a magnet stand to pellet the beads. Recover the supernatant that contains the DNA to a new tube. Purify the DNA with 0.5x volume of AMPure XP SPRI beads, wash twice with 500 Â”L 70% ethanol. Elute in 20 Â”L H 2 O. QC with Quant-it for concentration, run BioAnalyzer DNA 12000 assay (Agilent) or 1% agarose gel to visualize the size distribution. The majority of the fragments should be > 10kb. Rolling circle amplified DNA trace with BioAnalyzer DNA 12000 assay Rolling circle amplified DNA on a 1% agarose gel; the top band of the ladder is 10kb. The elongated cDNA is now ready for downstream Oxford Nanopore Technologies (ONT) library prep.
From this point on, be really careful with the DNA since it will shear pretty easily. Do not pipet up and down and try not to vortex.
creator: xian Adiconis
